description: Operator overview for the openZro mesh control plane — management gRPC, signal exchange, relay fabric, IdP traffic and per-component resource usage.

options.content:
openZro · Control Plane
Real-time health and throughput of the openZro mesh: management gRPC, signal exchange, relay fabric, IdP traffic, and per-component resource usage. Filter by namespace and pod using the variables above.

panels.title: Connected peers (mgmt)
panels.description: Active gRPC sync streams to management — i.e. peers currently online from the control plane's point of view.

panels.title: Active peers (signal)
panels.description: Peers currently registered on signal pods. Should track connected peers closely; a wide divergence indicates signal/management drift.

panels.title: Active relay sessions
panels.description: Sum of peer sessions across relay pods. Peers fall back to relay when direct WireGuard handshake fails.

panels.title: Sync requests / s
panels.description: Per-second rate of GetNetworkMap / Sync calls. Each connected peer should produce roughly one stream open per reconnect, not a steady request rate — sustained values indicate peers are flapping.

panels.title: Management pods up
panels.description: Number of management pods reporting `up=1` via Prometheus.

panels.title: Login error rate
panels.description: Login errors as a fraction of total login attempts over 5m. Sustained non-zero values flag broken IdP federation or expired secrets.

panels.title: Login & registration rate
panels.description: Rate of inbound peer registrations (`GetServerKey` then `Login`). Spikes after rollouts or token rotations are normal; sustained spikes indicate peers reconnecting in a loop.

panels.title: Sync stream open rate
panels.description: Rate at which peers open new bidirectional sync streams. Should match reconnect rate, not steady traffic — every long-lived stream sustains many network-map pushes without re-opening.

panels.title: Login latency (p50 / p95 / p99)
panels.description: End-to-end `Login` duration in milliseconds. p99 above ~500ms usually means the IdP round-trip or store write is the bottleneck.

panels.title: Sync latency (p50 / p95 / p99)
panels.description: Time spent inside the `Sync` handler (first response). Reflects network-map computation and update-channel dispatch.

panels.title: Active peers per signal pod
panels.description: Concurrent peers registered on each signal pod. Stacked sum should match the mgmt-side `connected streams` count.

panels.title: Messages forwarded / s
panels.description: Rate of relay-of-relays SDP messages. Each handshake produces a handful of forwards; sustained high values mean lots of new tunnels.

panels.title: Registrations vs deregistrations / s
panels.description: Rate of peers joining / leaving the signal plane. A clean shutdown deregisters; force kills produce orphan sessions cleaned by timeout.

panels.title: Peer connection duration p95
panels.description: How long peers stay connected to signal before tearing down. Very short durations (<30s) suggest a reconnect storm.

panels.title: Relay peers (active vs idle)
panels.description: Active sessions (currently exchanging traffic) vs idle (connected but quiet). Idle counts spike during overnight lulls.

panels.title: Relay throughput (sent + received)
panels.description: Aggregate bytes/sec flowing through every relay pod. Sent and received should be near-equal at steady state.

panels.title: Reconnection rate (mesh stability)
panels.description: Peers re-establishing relay sessions. High values mean the mesh is unstable — investigate network drops, pod restarts, or LB stickiness.

panels.title: Cluster forwards / s
panels.description: Cross-replica forwards inside the relay fabric. Non-zero values prove peers across pods can reach each other; zero with multi-replica is a smoking gun for broken cluster wiring.

panels.title: Cluster pings lost / s
panels.description: Heartbeats between relay replicas that never came back. Sustained non-zero means split-brain risk — check the cluster backend (NATS / Redis) and inter-pod connectivity.

panels.title: Update channel send latency
panels.description: How long it takes to push a network-map update into a peer's send channel. p99 above ~1ms means we're saturating Go channels — usually a sign of a slow consumer (peer that can't keep up).

panels.title: NetworkMap calc duration
panels.description: Time spent computing the per-peer NetworkMap. Grows ~O(accounts × peers); if p95 climbs without peer growth, look for new policy complexity.

panels.title: Store persistence latency
panels.description: Duration of writes to the management store (Postgres / MySQL / SQLite). Sustained high p99 is a database problem, not a Go problem.

panels.title: Account & network-map cardinality
panels.description: Total peers per network map and how many peer-meta updates we record. Grows linearly with account size.

panels.title: IdP traffic (requests vs errors)
panels.description: Calls to the configured IdP (Dex by default in openZro). Errors include JWKS fetches, token introspection, and user-info lookups.

panels.title: Store transaction duration
panels.description: End-to-end transaction time. Compares against persistence latency to show how much of the trip is lock contention / planner overhead.

panels.title: Memory per pod (RSS)
panels.description: `container_memory_working_set_bytes` per pod. Compare with the chart's `resources.limits.memory` to detect approaching OOM kills.

panels.title: CPU per pod
panels.description: CPU cores used per pod. A pod pinned at its limit will throttle — see the throttling panel below.

panels.title: Pod restart count (cumulative)
panels.description: Sum of restarts across all containers in each pod. Should stay flat — any rise is worth investigating.

panels.title: Goroutines per process
panels.description: Go runtime goroutine count. Linear growth without bounds is a leak — typically a goroutine waiting on a channel that nobody closes.

panels.title: CPU throttling (5m)
panels.description: Fraction of CPU time the container was throttled. Anything above ~5% means the limit is too tight for the workload.

targets.expr:
sum(rate(container_cpu_cfs_throttled_periods_total{namespace="$namespace", pod=~"$pod", container!="", container!="POD"}[5m])) by (pod)
/
clamp_min(sum(rate(container_cpu_cfs_periods_total{namespace="$namespace", pod=~"$pod", container!="", container!="POD"}[5m])) by (pod), 1)

title: openZro · Control Plane Overview
